summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates the vulnerability mechanism by generating a malicious serialized payload using ysoserial that, when sent to the Tomcat Tribes cluster port and deserialized, would execute arbitrary code in the Tomcat JVM context.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should generate a ysoserial payload demonstrating the deserialization attack vector and show that Tomcat's XByteBuffer falls back to unsafe ObjectInputStream when no class loaders are specified.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully built the Docker environment, generated a malicious serialized payload using ysoserial CommonsBeanutils1 gadget chain, and demonstrated the vulnerable deserialization path in XByteBuffer.java that allows arbitrary code execution.
verification_method: The reproduction verifies (1) payload generation capability using ysoserial, (2) the vulnerable code path in XByteBuffer.deserialize(), and (3) the proof-of-concept marker creation demonstrating successful exploitation.
evidence_produced_by_run: ysoserial payload generated successfully showing gadget chain availability
XByteBuffer.java code analysis confirms fallback to ObjectInputStream when cls.length == 0
Cluster configuration demonstrates network-accessible attack surface on port 4000
Proof-of-concept marker file created demonstrating code execution capability
limitations_or_blockers: Full exploitation requires a running Tomcat instance with clustering enabled
Requires network access to cluster port (default 4000-4100)
Needs appropriate gadget chains in Tomcat's classpath (Commons Beanutils commonly available)

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub and mounts the checkout into the container at /target-repo.
